financial.note: BBL is the primary credit provider for the group; following the 3BB disposal, JAS maintains significant cash but remains sensitive to interest rates for JTS expansion capital.
Holds ~50.31% of shares; his personal liquidity and strategic direction are the primary drivers of JAS's pivot into crypto-assets and AI.

supply_chain.note: ASIC miners and GPUs are the core CAPEX for JTS; price spikes in hardware or supply chain delays in 2026 impact mining hash-rate growth.
Electricity represents the largest OPEX for Bitcoin mining operations; 2026 Ft rate fluctuations directly impact bottom-line profitability.
JAS HK secured 2026 Premier League rights in Vietnam; success depends on downstream sub-licensing and subscriber growth in the region.

competitors.note: Direct competition in the domestic Bitcoin mining sector for power resources and investor capital.
Competes in the data center and enterprise cloud space following JAS's shift toward high-performance computing.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: Direct correlation to the valuation of JTS and the primary revenue stream for the group's new core business.
ASIC hardware and GPU imports are USD-denominated; THB weakness inflates CAPEX costs for mining and AI infrastructure.
Affects the discount rate for future cash flows in the long-term data center and media rights segments.
